claimText: Lenacapavir is an antiretroviral medicine used as Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP) for the prevention of HIV.
evidenceExcerpt: Lenacapavir is an antiretroviral medicine used as Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP) for HIV prevention.
examUse: Statement-based question on recent medical developments or correct-pair matching of drug to disease.
distractorHooks: Stating Lenacapavir is a vaccine rather than an antiretroviral drug
Confusing Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP) with a permanent cure

claimText: HIV weakens the immune system by specifically infecting and destroying CD4 T lymphocytes, a type of white blood cell.
evidenceExcerpt: HIV attacks the immune system by destroying CD4 T lymphocytes (white blood cells).
examUse: Statement-based question on the mechanism of viral diseases.
distractorHooks: Claiming HIV attacks red blood cells or platelets
Confusing CD4 T lymphocytes with CD8 T cells or B cells

claimText: HIV can be transmitted from an infected mother to her baby.
evidenceExcerpt: It transmits through body fluids and from mother to baby.
examUse: Statement-based question on modes of disease transmission.
distractorHooks: Asserting that HIV cannot be transmitted from mother to child
Claiming HIV transmits through casual contact or air

claimText: Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) infections are treated using antiretroviral therapy (ART).
examUse: Statement-based question on treatment protocols for viral diseases.
distractorHooks: Stating HIV is treated with antibiotics
Claiming ART is a preventive vaccine rather than a treatment
